notes.title: Read these files for context:
notes.body:
Read these files for context:
- polymarket-bot/docs/multi_strategy_architecture.md
- polymarket-bot/strategies/strategy_manager.py
- polymarket-bot/strategies/spread_arb.py
- polymarket-bot/strategies/weather_trader.py
- polymarket-bot/paper_runner.py
- polymarket-bot/ideas.txt

Build the complete self-improving trading system:

1. RBI Pipeline (polymarket-bot/strategies/rbi_pipeline.py):
   - Monitor ideas.txt for "pending" entries
   - For each: run 4-hour paper backtest using paper_runner infrastructure
   - If positive P/L after fees ($0.05 gas, 2% winner tax, 0.5% slippage): mark "validated", notify via Redis notifications:trading
   - If negative: mark "rejected" with data
   - Auto-promote: if an idea validates 3 times consecutively, add it to live strategies
   - Async loop, checks every 30 min
   - CLI: python rbi_pipeline.py --idea "name" --hours 4

2. Order Flow / CVD Divergence Detector (polymarket-bot/strategies/cvd_detector.py):
   - Inspired by @zostaff's CVD bot: price goes up but money flows out = sell, price drops but money flows in = buy
   - Monitor Polymarket CLOB orderbook for volume/price divergence
   - Track cumulative volume delta per market over 15-min windows
   - When divergence exceeds threshold (price moved >5% but CVD is opposite sign): generate signal
   - Log signals, publish to Redis for paper trader to act on
   - This is strategy #3 in the multi-strategy architecture

3. Wire strategy_manager.py to actually start all 3 strategies:
   - Weather cheap bracket (40% bankroll)
   - Filtered copytrade (35% bankroll) 
   - CVD/arb combined (25% bankroll)
   - SharedPositionRegistry prevents overlap
   - Correlation monitoring every 15 min
   - Hourly P/L dashboard via iMessage
   - Update polymarket-bot/src/main.py to initialize StrategyManager and start all strategies

4. Intel feeds auto-feeding ideas:
   - When integrations/intel_feeds/signal_aggregator.py detects a high-relevance signal (score >80), auto-create an entry in ideas.txt with status "pending"
   - The RBI pipeline picks it up automatically
   - Complete the loop: intel finds idea   paper tests it   winner goes live

Use standard logging (not structlog) for anything called from imessage-server.py. Redis at redis://172.18.0.100:6379 inside Docker, redis://localhost:6379 on host.

Commit each major piece separately. Push to origin main. {kv 'CM8

notes.title: Polymarket
notes.body:
Polymarket

Go Live

cd ~/AI-Server
sed -i '' 's/POLY_DRY_RUN=true/POLY_DRY_RUN=false/' .env
sed -i '' 's/KRAKEN_DRY_RUN=true/KRAKEN_DRY_RUN=false/' .env
docker compose up -d --force-recreate polymarket-bot

text
docker logs -f polymarket-bot 2>&1 | grep "copytrade_copy_executed\|copytrade_new_trade\|copytrade_skip\|copytrade_order_error\|copytrade_rate_adjusted\|bankroll_updated"
That shows the live feed of trades being copied, skipped, errors, and bankroll/rate changes.
A few other useful ones:
Check current status (positions, bankroll, circuit breaker):

text
curl -s http://localhost:8430/status | python3 -m json.tool
See just errors:

text
docker logs polymarket-bot --tail 50 2>&1 | grep "error"
Check if notification-hub is alive and routing to iMessage:

text
docker logs notification-hub --tail 20
Check all open positions:

text
docker logs polymarket-bot --tail 200 2>&1 | grep "copytrade_position\|exit_engine"

notes.title: You are building the first working backend for a product called:
notes.body:
GYou are building the first working backend for a product called:

IOI (In Out In)

Core Concept:
IOI is a real-time physical awareness system that tracks objects moving through space, primarily through doorway  threshold  scanners and optional interior zones. The system continuously maintains a live, intelligent state of where important items are, what they re associated with (kits), and whether anything is missing, misplaced, or behaving abnormally.

This is NOT a basic tracking app. It is the foundation of a new system layer:
  A digital twin of real-world objects and their movement.

The name IOI comes from:
  In   Out   In
This represents the fundamental loop of object movement across thresholds (doors, zones, environments).

The system should be designed as an event-driven, stateful platform that can scale into:
- Smart homes
- Job sites
- Travel systems
- Security systems
- Inventory intelligence

--------------------------------------------------

TECH STACK (REQUIRED)

- Node.js (latest LTS)
- TypeScript
- Fastify (API framework)
- PostgreSQL (primary DB)
- Prisma ORM
- Redis (event buffering + caching)
- WebSockets (real-time updates)
- Zod (validation)
- Pino (logging)
- Vitest (testing)
- Docker + Docker Compose
- ESLint + Prettier

--------------------------------------------------

ARCHITECTURE REQUIREMENTS

This must be built like a real system, not a CRUD demo.

Use layered architecture:
- API layer (routes/controllers)
- Services (business logic)
- Domain layer (movement + state logic)
- Repositories (DB access)
- Event pipeline (raw   interpreted)
- State projection layer
- Alert engine

Design it so it can evolve into:
- hardware integrations (RFID/NFC readers)
- Home Assistant / Control4 integration
- mobile apps
- cloud or edge deployments

--------------------------------------------------

CORE SYSTEM MODEL

The system tracks:

1. Objects (Items)
2. Tags (RFID/NFC identifiers)
3. Zones (physical locations)
4. Readers (devices detecting tags)
5. Movements (interpreted events)
6. State (current known truth)
7. Kits (expected item groupings)
8. Alerts (behavior anomalies)

--------------------------------------------------

DOMAIN ENTITIES

Create a full Prisma schema including:

User
Household
Zone
Reader
Item
Tag
Kit
KitItem
RawReadEvent
MovementEvent
ItemState
AlertRule
AlertEvent
WebhookEndpoint

--------------------------------------------------

ENUMS

ZoneType:
- DOORWAY
- STORAGE
- PERSONAL
- VEHICLE
- UNKNOWN

ReaderType:
- RFID_PORTAL
- RFID_ZONE
- NFC_READER
- MANUAL

ItemStatus:
- HOME
- OUT
- MISPLACED
- UNKNOWN

MovementType:
- ENTERED_ZONE
- EXITED_ZONE
- MOVED_ZONE
- LEFT_HOME
- RETURNED_HOME

AlertSeverity:
- INFO
- WARNING
- CRITICAL

ItemPriority:
- LOW
- NORMAL
- HIGH
- ESSENTIAL

--------------------------------------------------

CORE BEHAVIOR (IOI LOGIC)

This is the most important part.

The system revolves around IOI transitions:

IN   OUT   IN

Rules:

1. Doorway reader detects direction:
   - OUT   i

notes.title: Zoho Setup
notes.body:
Zoho Setup

Fastest Setup (all devices)
Use these Zoho settings:
IMAP server: imap.zoho.com (SSL, port 993)
SMTP server: smtp.zoho.com (TLS/SSL, port 465 587)
Username: full email address
Password: account password or app password (required if 2FA is on)
Device by device
iPhone/iPad: Settings   Mail   Accounts   Add Account   Other   Add Mail Account (or Zoho if shown), then enter IMAP/SMTP above.
Mac Mail: Mail   Settings   Accounts   Add Account   Other Mail Account.
Outlook: Add Account   Advanced setup   IMAP.
Gmail app: Add account   Other (IMAP).
If you mean  import old mailbox into Zoho 
Use Zoho s migration tools (one-time):
Zoho Mail Admin Console  Data Migration
Source: Gmail/Outlook/IMAP/PST, etc.
Run migration once; then all devices just sync from Zoho normally.
Important gotchas
Enable IMAP in Zoho Mail settings first.
If 2FA enabled, generate a Zoho app password for each mail app.
Keep  Leave messages on server  enabled (default with IMAP).
If you want, I can give you the exact click-by-click for your specific devices (e.g., iPhone + Mac + Outlook Windows) in one checklist.

notes.title: Home assistant
notes.body:
Home assistant 
admin
Homeassist247!
 
Ring password - 
event.front_door_motion

service: automation.trigger
target:
  entity_id: automation.save_snapshot_backyard

service: automation.trigger
target:
  entity_id: automation.save_snapshot_driveway

service: automation.trigger
target:
  entity_id: automation.save_snapshot_garage

service: automation.trigger
target:
  entity_id: automation.save_snapshot_front_door

watch -n 1 ls -l /config/www/ring_snapshots/

(Optional) One-button deploy you can keep using

If you don t already have it, this validates   restarts   commits any local edits and pushes:

cat > /config/deploy.sh <<'EOF'
#!/usr/bin/env sh
set -e
cd /config
./check_c4_ring.sh
ha core restart
sleep 10
git add -A
git commit -m "deploy: $(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")" || true
if git remote get-url origin >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  git push -u origin $(git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD)
  git push --tags || true
fi
echo "Deploy complete."
EOF
chmod +x /config/deploy.sh

# On your Pi:
ls -la /config/www/daily_history/
ls -la /config/www/daily_history/$(date +%Y%m%d)/


Current Snapshots (Always Fresh)

notes.title: Got it — everything below is plain text and fully copy-pasteable…
notes.body:
Got it   everything below is plain text and fully copy-pasteable.
I ll give you clean, standalone descriptions for EACH device, written so they still push the bundle first and reduce the risk of being stuck with one item.

You can paste these directly into OfferUp, Facebook Marketplace, Craigslist, etc.

 

==============================
RAZER CORE X   INDIVIDUAL DESCRIPTION

Title:
Razer Core X Thunderbolt 3 eGPU Enclosure   650W PSU

Price:
$350 OBO (Bundle Preferred)

Description:
Razer Core X Thunderbolt 3 external GPU enclosure in excellent working condition. This enclosure allows laptops and Macs with Thunderbolt 3 to run full-size desktop graphics cards.

  Thunderbolt 3 connectivity
  Built-in 650W power supply
  Supports full-length, full-height GPUs
  Quiet, stable, and well-ventilated
  Clean condition, smoke-free environment

This unit has been fully tested and works perfectly. It was previously used as part of a complete eGPU setup and is confirmed compatible with the AMD Vega Frontier Edition also listed.

Strongly prefer selling as part of the bundle to avoid parting out a proven setup.

Local pickup preferred. Can demo for serious buyers.

 

==============================
AMD VEGA FRONTIER EDITION   INDIVIDUAL DESCRIPTION

Title:
AMD Radeon Vega Frontier Edition   16GB HBM2 Workstation GPU

Price:
$400 OBO (Bundle Preferred)

Description:
AMD Radeon Vega Frontier Edition professional graphics card with 16GB of HBM2 VRAM. This is the workstation-class Vega card, not the consumer gaming version.

  16GB HBM2 memory
  Excellent for video editing, 3D, CAD, and compute workloads
  Stable, reliable, and runs clean
  Fully tested and working
  Never mined or abused

This GPU was used in a Thunderbolt eGPU setup with a Razer Core X and worked flawlessly. It s a great option for macOS, Linux, or workstation builds.

Strong preference to sell as part of the full eGPU bundle.

Local pickup preferred.

 

==============================
PRIMARY BUNDLE LISTING (USE THIS AS MAIN POST)

Title:
Complete Thunderbolt eGPU Bundle   Razer Core X + Vega Frontier 16GB

Price:
$700 Firm (Bundle Preferred)

Description:
Selling a complete, ready-to-use Thunderbolt eGPU setup consisting of:

  Razer Core X Thunderbolt 3 enclosure (650W PSU)
  AMD Radeon Vega Frontier Edition with 16GB HBM2 VRAM

This is a matched, tested bundle   not random parts. Everything has been used together and confirmed working.

Ideal for:
  Mac eGPU setups
  Video editing
  3D / CAD
  Compute-heavy workloads

Clean condition, smoke-free environment.
Upgraded my workstation and no longer need this setup.

Bundle strongly preferred. Will only consider splitting if both items are spoken for.

Local pickup preferred. Demo available for serious buyers.

 

==============================
COPY-PASTE RESPONSE FOR SPLIT REQUESTS

 m prioritizing selling this as a bundle since it s a proven setup. If that changes, I ll reach back out. 

==============================
COPY-PASTE RESPONSE F

notes.title: Willows 305 Programming
notes.body:
4Willows 305 Programming

Macro (per room): <Room>_UpdateAudioLEDs

If Room <Room> Power is OFF
    Use the on color for Area Off on <Room>-><KP>
    Use the off color for Spotify on <Room>-><KP>
    Use the off color for TV Audio on <Room>-><KP>
    Use the off color for Play/Pause on <Room>-><KP>
    Stop
End If

If Room <Room> Selected Audio Device is TV Audio
    Use the on color for TV Audio on <Room>-><KP>
    Use the off color for Spotify on <Room>-><KP>
    // TV path  paused  = Mute ON
    If Equipment Closet->Triad 8x8 Audio Matrix Switch Audio Output <Out> [<Room>] is Muted
        Set Play/Pause LED on the <Room>-><KP> to Red
    Else
        Use the off color for Play/Pause on <Room>-><KP>
    End If
    Stop
End If

// Music path (use the flag)
Use the off color for TV Audio on <Room>-><KP>
Use the on color for Spotify on <Room>-><KP>
If Variables -> <Room>_Playing equals False
    Set Play/Pause LED on the <Room>-><KP> to Red
Else
    Use the off color for Play/Pause on <Room>-><KP>
End If
 
Programming   <Room> - Control   Room Turned Off
Variables -> Set <Room>_Playing = False
Execute Macro <Room>_UpdateAudioLEDs
 
Programming   <Room>    Events   Audio Selection Changed

Variables -> Set <Room>_Playing = False
Execute Macro <Room>_UpdateAudioLEDs
 
KITCHEN (Left KP, Output 1)

Play/Pause   Single Tap

If Room Kitchen Selected Audio Device is TV Audio
    Equipment Closet->Triad 8x8 Audio Matrix Switch Toggle Mute on Audio Output 1 [Kitchen]
    If Equipment Closet->Triad 8x8 Audio Matrix Switch Audio Output 1 [Kitchen] is Muted
        Set Play/Pause LED on the Kitchen->Left KP to Red
    Else
        Use the off color for Play/Pause on Kitchen->Left KP
    End If
    Use the on color for TV Audio on Kitchen->Left KP
    Use the off color for Spotify on Kitchen->Left KP
Else
    If Variables -> Kitchen_Playing equals True
        [Kitchen Selected Audio Device] -> Pause
        Variables -> Set Kitchen_Playing = False
        Set Play/Pause LED on the Kitchen->Left KP to Red
    Else
        If Room Kitchen Room Power is OFF
            Agents -> Media Scenes -> Execute Scene "Kitchen Preset"
        End If
        [Kitchen Selected Audio Device] -> Play
        Variables -> Set Kitchen_Playing = True
        Use the off color for Play/Pause on Kitchen->Left KP
    End If
    Use the off color for TV Audio on Kitchen->Left KP
    Use the on color for Spotify on Kitchen->Left KP
End If
Execute Macro Kitchen_UpdateAudioLEDs
 
HALL (Right KP, Output 7)

Play/Pause   Single Tap

If Room Hall Selected Audio Device is TV Audio
    Equipment Closet->Triad 8x8 Audio Matrix Switch Toggle Mute on Audio Output 7 [Hall]
    If Equipment Closet->Triad 8x8 Audio Matrix Switch Audio Output 7 [Hall] is Muted
        Set Play/Pause LED on the Hall->Right KP to Red
    Else
        Use the off color for Play/Pause on Hall->Right KP
    End If
    Use the on color for TV Audio on Hall->Right KP
    Use the off color for Spotify on Hall->Right KP
E
notes.summary: 4Willows 305 Programming  Macro (per room): <Room>_UpdateAudioLEDs  If Room <Room> Power is OFF     Use the on color for Area Off on <Room>-><KP>     Use the off color for Spotify on <Room>-><KP>

notes.title: Plex Server restart
notes.body:
Plex Server restart

ssh Matt@192.168.1.91

Password1


Hard restart

killall -9 "Plex Media Server" 2>/dev/null || true
sleep 1
ps aux | grep -i "Plex Media Server" | grep -v grep || true

Verify its working

"/share/CACHEDEV1_DATA/.qpkg/PlexMediaServer/Plex Media Server" &
sleep 3
curl -sI http://127.0.0.1:32400/identity || wget -qO- http://127.0.0.1:32400/identity

Auto start

setcfg PlexMediaServer Enable TRUE -f /etc/config/qpkg.conf
/etc/init.d/qpkg.sh start PlexMediaServer

Manual Start

/sbin/qpkg_cli --stop PlexMediaServer

/sbin/qpkg_cli --start PlexMediaServer

/sbin/qpkg_cli --status PlexMediaServer


Restart Plex(one-liner)

/sbin/qpkg_cli --stop PlexMediaServer && sleep 5 && /sbin/qpkg_cli --start PlexMediaServer && /sbin/qpkg_cli --status PlexMediaServer

Whenever you make local changes (new chats, script edits, etc.):

cd "/Users/Matt/Library/Mobile Documents/com~apple~CloudDocs/Symphony SH/Outline Creator"
git add .
git commit -m "Update outline and auto-refresh structure"
git push

notes.title: CSV MAKER
notes.body:
CSV MAKER

# Quick check if agent is running
alias intake-status='launchctl list | grep auto_intake'

# Watch logs
alias intake-logs='tail -f /tmp/auto_intake.log'

# Stop agent
alias intake-stop="launchctl unload ~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.symphonysh.auto_intake.plist"

# Start agent
alias intake-start="launchctl load ~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.symphonysh.auto_intake.plist && launchctl start com.symphonysh.auto_intake"

Move around
Arrows work, but h (left), l (right), j (down), k (up) are faster once you get used to them.
w jumps by word, 0 goes to line start, $ goes to line end.
Select inside brackets/quotes
Place your cursor anywhere inside <...>.
Type: vi<   highlights everything inside < >.
Type: va<   highlights the whole thing including the brackets.
Change contents quickly
ci<  change inside < >  = deletes text inside and puts you into insert mode.
ca<  change around < >  = deletes text including brackets.
Then just type your replacement and press Esc.
Undo/redo
u = undo.
Ctrl + r = redo.
	
	
	:wq = save and quit.
	:q! = quit without saving.
	:w = save without quitting.


Efficiency tip
For your plist edits, the fastest flow is:
Move cursor into < >.
Hit ci<.
Paste or type the new value.
Hit Esc.
Save & quit with :wq.
That replaces the whole value in one move, no dragging/selecting.


Shortcuts

open_urls.sh - opens to urls folder

open_pdfs.sh - opens to pdfs folder

add_url.sh 


Quick nano moves (so you fly)
	 	Go to line/column: Ctrl+_ then type LINE,COLUMN (e.g., 120,1).
	 	Search / next / prev: Ctrl+W, then Alt+W / Alt+Q.
	 	Toggle soft wrap (if needed): Alt+L.
	 	Cut line / paste: Ctrl+K / Ctrl+U.
	 	Save / exit: Ctrl+O / Ctrl+X.

bash <<'SH'
set -euo pipefail

NANORC="$HOME/.nanorc"

# Collect all available nano syntax files (handles macOS & Homebrew paths)
INCLUDES=""
for d in /opt/homebrew/share/nano /usr/local/share/nano /usr/share/nano; do
  if [ -d "$d" ]; then
    for f in "$d"/*.nanorc; do
      [ -e "$f" ] || continue
      INCLUDES="${INCLUDES}\ninclude \"$f\""
    done
  fi
done

# Write ~/.nanorc with readable UI + line numbers + includes
cat > "$NANORC" <<EOF
## ===== Nano UI & Usability =====
set linenumbers         ## show line numbers
set constantshow        ## show cursor position in the status bar
set softwrap            ## wrap long lines gently
set tabsize 2           ## tabs show as 2 spaces
set autoindent          ## keep indent on new lines
set smarthome           ## Home toggles line start/first non-blank
set mouse               ## allow basic mouse support
set zap                 ## ^Delete deletes next char (like many editors)
set historylog          ## remember search/replace history

## Colors (tweak if you want higher contrast)
set titlecolor brightwhite,blue
set statuscolor brightwhite,magenta
set selectedcolor black,cyan
set numbercolor green
set functioncolor brightwhite,black

## ===== Syntax Highlighting =====
$INCLUDES
EOF

echo "  Wrote $NANORC"

# Quick test file to open right away
printf '%s\n

notes.title: Rosecrown:
notes.body:
Rosecrown:

-Control 4 will be installed throughout the house to cover for the devices being installed now and any add ons wanted into the future
-Lighting Integration - all Lutron keypads will be replaced with C4 centralized keypads and wall stations that power the lights will be replaced with Adaptive Phase Dimmers (some drywall damage may apply but should be minimal)
-Security Cameras - will get power to the current outdoor cameras and can possibly repurpose some of the other indoor cameras not in use, no new cameras in the proposal. 
-WiFi Door Lock - not in the proposal but can look into options 
-Thermostats - have to confirm there is wiring behind all stats to support C4, but am looking into other brands that we can easily tie into C4 via other options like WiFi/Zigbee)
-Music Integration - all Sonos will be replaced, so no more cutting in and out. All zones are covered and I believe I may have added a new zone for the trampoline/upper yard area
-Pool Sound - all current speakers will be replaced with landscape speakers
-Wifi 6 (Indoor/Outdoor) - using the current network in the Loft Closet, along with new wiring that will be ran outside to get the rest of the house on proper wiring expected in 2025, which includes a double strand multi mode fiber and Cat6. Araknis WiFi 6 4x4 APs to give you multi gig speeds as they become available, and to create a strong network no matter where you are in the house or out by the pool, and no matter how many people are at the house
-Conduit Where Needed - possible conduit if needed but I think I can hide wire between the gutters or under the eaves. 
-Ipads - not in quote but can add them above any outlet around the house, if needed, as phones are the go to, and the new C4 keypads should have you covered for all lighting needs
-Coil TV Cables Behind Units - living room TV is quoted with a back box to move everything hanging out on the floor behind the TV for a clean look.  b*H` rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah rah

notes.title: Singita Summit Reset Steps
notes.body:
Singita Summit Reset Steps
 
Front Entry Closet - turn off both power strips
 
Exercise closet - turn off power strip
 
Crawlspace - Unplug top plugs
 
Rec Bedroom - unplug both
 
Unplug 3 devices on top of crates
Wait 30 seconds
From left to right plug in each device but wait until you see WAN lights on left(araknis router) start blinking, then plug eero in, wait for white solid light, then plug the switch in 
Turn power strip in exercise closet back on
Plug back in extension cords in crawlspace
Wait 30 seconds and plug in Rec Bedroom devices
Turn back on power strip in front entry closet $(; '(F )(Q *(] b*H` m*  H(h H(h H(h H(h H(h H(h H(h H(h H(h H(h A(A b3

notes.title: Cleerline Fiber
notes.body:
Cleerline Fiber

Module 1

Non-Conductive which makes it immune to EMI, RFI, and ground loop interference. It is also infected by lighting strikes
Non corrosive
More affordable
Lightweight
More durable
Pull tolerance - 225lbs(10x the amount of 6A)

Distance - OM4
Multimode - 100Gb - 500ft(150M)
Single Mode - 1.2Pb - Several Miles

Latency -  time it takes a data packet to travel from its source to its destination

Module 2

Core - light carrying element
Cladding - pure silica - density causes light to reflect within the core when it encounters the claddING
Coating
First layer - primary buffer - 250 micron hard acrylate coating
Second - tight buffer - 900 micron layer
Strengthening Fibres to provide strain relief
Cable jacket - colored to an industry standard

2 types of fiber - single mode and multimode

Single mode - small core - 8-10 microns - used for long distance - infinite bandwidth - transmits single light ray - any core minimizes dispersion which allows higher bandwidth signals to be sent over longer distances - 300M(1000ft)

Multimode - 50-62 microns - thicker core allows multiple modes(rays) of light to be transmitted simultaneously - each light beam follows its own path leads to modal dispersion which Creates limits on both distance and capacity 

Fiber size - first number represents the core diameter and the second number represents the cladding diameter

Modulation - the process of converting data by adding it to an electronic or optical signal
 
Bandwidth and Distance

MM - larger core - greater margin of error

SM - smaller core - best and only solution for longer distance applications

notes.title: Beef Jerky Info
notes.body:
Beef Jerky Info
 
Steak purchase

Local Options:
1.	Edwards Meats: This local butcher shop offers a wide selection of beef cuts, including eye of round. They pride themselves on providing top-quality meats sourced from Nebraska, featuring grass-fed and grain-finished beef.  
https://edwardsmeats.com/meats/beef/

2.	Natural Beef Colorado: Located in Colorado, they offer various beef cuts, including eye of round steak at $14.50 per pound and eye of round roast at $6.50 per pound.  
https://www.naturalbeef.com/price.html

Online Options:
1.	Bay s Meat Market: They offer eye of round steak at $11.00 per pound, available for online ordering.  
https://baysmeatmarket.com/product/eye-of-round-steak/

2.	Smith s Food and Drug: They provide a value pack of beef choice eye of round roast (two roasts per pack) at $7.49 per pound. Availability may vary based on location.  
https://www.smithsfoodanddrug.com/p/beef-choice-eye-of-round-roast-value-pack-2-roasts-per-pack-/0020229400000

________________________________________
 Recipes

1. Turmeric-Ginger Marinade (Anti-Inflammatory & Gut Healing)

This marinade combines anti-inflammatory turmeric and ginger with coconut aminos for a deep umami flavor.

Ingredients
  cup coconut aminos (gluten-free alternative to soy sauce)
 	2 tbsp apple cider vinegar (helps tenderize the meat)
 	1 tbsp fresh grated ginger (anti-inflammatory)
 	1 tbsp fresh minced garlic (immune-boosting)
 	1 tsp turmeric powder (reduces inflammation)
 	1 tsp black pepper (enhances turmeric absorption)
 	1 tsp smoked paprika (adds depth)
  tsp cinnamon (helps balance blood sugar)
  tsp Himalayan or Celtic salt
  tsp crushed red pepper flakes (optional for heat)
  Instructions: Mix ingredients, marinate beef slices for at least 6 hours, then dehydrate.

2. Lemon-Garlic Herb Marinade (Bright & Clean Flavor)

A refreshing, Mediterranean-inspired mix with citrus and herbs.

Ingredients:
  cup olive oil (healthy fat, anti-inflammatory)
  cup fresh lemon juice
 	2 tbsp coconut aminos
 	1 tbsp apple cider vinegar
 	1 tbsp fresh minced garlic
 	1 tbsp oregano (rich in antioxidants)
 	1 tsp thyme (anti-microbial)
 	1 tsp rosemary (helps digestion)
  tsp black pepper
  tsp sea salt
  Instructions: Marinate for 6 12 hours before drying.

3. Smoky Maple Balsamic Marinade (Sweet & Savory)

A deep, smoky flavor with natural sweetness from maple syrup.

Ingredients:
  cup coconut aminos
 	2 tbsp balsamic vinegar (low acidity, full of polyphenols)
 	1 tbsp pure maple syrup (natural, lower glycemic impact)
 	1 tbsp garlic powder
 	1 tsp smoked paprika
 	1 tsp ground mustard (boosts metabolism)
  tsp cayenne (optional for a little heat)
  tsp sea salt
  tsp black pepper
  Instructions: Let the beef soak for 6 12 hours before drying.

4. Spicy Cilantro-Lime Marinade (Mexican-Inspired)

A zesty, tangy option with fresh herbs.

Ingredients:
  cup coconut aminos
  cup fresh lime juice
 	2 tbsp olive oil
 	2 tbsp fresh chopped cilantro
 	1 tbsp fresh minced garlic

#!/usr/bin/env python3
import textwrap
from reportlab.pdfgen import canvas
from reportlab.lib.pagesizes import letter

def create_invoice_form(filename, num_rows, include_aging):
    c = canvas.Canvas(filename, pagesize=letter)
    width, height = letter

    left_margin = 50
    right_margin = 50

    # ------------------------------------------------
    # 1. Header: Company Text Block and Left-Aligned Logo
    # ------------------------------------------------
    # Define the top position for the text block.
    text_block_top = height - 50  # 50 pts from the top

    # Company text block: company name and details.
    company_lines = [
        "Symphony Smart Homes",
        "45 Aspen Glen Ct",
        "Edwards, CO 81632",
        "(970)519-3013",
        "info@symphonysh.com",
        "symphonysh.com"
    ]
    
    # Define fonts and line heights.
    company_name_font = "Helvetica-Bold"
    company_name_size = 20
    details_font = "Helvetica"
    details_size = 10

    first_line_height = 20
    other_line_height = 12
    total_text_height = first_line_height + other_line_height * (len(company_lines) - 1)
    text_block_bottom = text_block_top - total_text_height

    # Draw the company text block (centered horizontally).
    # Draw the company name:
    c.setFont(company_name_font, company_name_size)
    c.drawCentredString(width/2, text_block_top - first_line_height + 5, company_lines[0])
    # Draw remaining lines:
    c.setFont(details_font, details_size)
    current_y = text_block_top - first_line_height
    for line in company_lines[1:]:
        c.drawCentredString(width/2, current_y - other_line_height + 5, line)
        current_y -= other_line_height

    # Compute vertical center of the text block.
    text_block_center = (text_block_top + text_block_bottom) / 2

    # Draw the logo left aligned.
    logo_path = "/Users/Matt/Library/Mobile Documents/com~apple~CloudDocs/Symphony SH/ssh scripts/Symphony_SH_Logo2.jpeg"
    logo_width = 100   # adjust as needed
    logo_height = total_text_height  # match the height of the text block
    logo_x = left_margin  # left aligned
    # Center the logo vertically on the text block.
    logo_y = text_block_center - (logo_height / 2)
    c.drawImage(logo_path, logo_x, logo_y, width=logo_width, height=logo_height, preserveAspectRatio=True, mask='auto')

    # ------------------------------------------------
    # 2. Aging Summary Section (Bottom) - Optional
    # ------------------------------------------------
    if include_aging:
        aging_bottom_y = 80  # y position near the bottom of the page
        available_width = width - left_margin - right_margin
        col_width = available_width / 6  # six columns
        aging_col_x = [left_margin + i * col_width for i in range(6)]
        
        aging_headings_line1 = ["Current", "1-30 Days", "31-60 Days", "61-90 Days", "90+ Days", "Amount"]
        aging_headings_line2 = ["Due", "Past Due", "Past Due",

Final Bot

"""
Crypto Trading Superbot v2.0
Features:
- GMGN API trading
- Solana MEV protection via Toxi
- Cross-chain security checks
- Unified risk management
"""

import os
import asyncio
from solana.rpc.async_api import AsyncClient
from solders.pubkey import Pubkey
from toxilib import ToxiTrader  # Toxi's Python SDK

class SuperTrader(GMGNTrader, EnhancedSecurity):
    def __init__(self):
        super().__init__()
        self.solana_client = AsyncClient(os.getenv("SOLANA_RPC_URL"))
        self.toxi = ToxiTrader(
            rpc_url=os.getenv("SOLANA_RPC_URL"),
            private_key=os.getenv("SOLANA_PRIVATE_KEY")
        )
        self.config.update({
            'max_slippage': 0.02,
            'mev_protection': True,
            'chain_preference': ['solana', 'evm']
        })

    async def execute_cross_chain_trade(self, symbol: str, strategy: str):
        """Unified trading interface"""
        # Security checks
        contract_address = await self.resolve_address(symbol)
        if not await self.full_security_scan(contract_address):
            return
            
        # Chain detection
        if await self.is_solana_token(symbol):
            await self.execute_solana_trade(symbol, strategy)
        else:
            await super().execute_trade(symbol, strategy)

    async def execute_solana_trade(self, symbol: str, strategy: str):
        """Toxi-integrated Solana trading"""
        token_info = await self.get_solana_token_info(symbol)
        
        # Toxi MEV-protected trade
        tx = await self.toxi.create_swap_transaction(
            input_mint=Pubkey.from_string(token_info['mint']),
            output_mint=Pubkey.from_string("So11111111111111111111111111111111111111112"),  # SOL
            amount=token_info['amount'],
            slippage=self.config['max_slippage'],
            mev_protection=self.config['mev_protection']
        )
        
        # Execute with Toxi's optimized router
        result = await self.toxi.send_transaction(tx)
        self.log_trade(symbol, result)

    async def full_security_scan(self, address: str) -> bool:
        """Cross-chain security verification"""
        checks = {
            'blacklist': not self.is_blacklisted(address),
            'rugcheck': await self.get_rugcheck_status(address) == 'Good',
            'holder_risk': not (await self.analyze_token_distribution(address))['is_bundled'],
            'solscore': await self.get_solana_security_score(address) > 75
        }
        
        return all(checks.values())

# Additional Solana-specific security
    async def get_solana_security_score(self, mint_address: str) -> float:
        """Check Solana token security metrics"""
        response = await self.solana_client.get_account_info_json_parsed(Pubkey.from_string(mint_address))
        data = response.value.data
        return data['security_score']  # From custom on-chain analysis

# Unified CLI
async def main():
    trader = SuperTrader()
    while True:
notes.summary: Final Bot  """ Crypto Trading Superbot v2.0 Features: - GMGN API trading - Solana MEV protection via Toxi - Cross-chain security checks - Unified risk management """  import os import asyncio from sol

notes.title: To install Ubuntu 22.04 LTS on your Mac Mini and replace macOS entirely…
notes.body:
To install Ubuntu 22.04 LTS on your Mac Mini and replace macOS entirely, follow these steps:
1. Prepare Your Mac Mini
Backup Data: Ensure all important data on your Mac is backed up, as the process will erase the entire disk.
Check for T2 Chip:
If your Mac Mini (2018 or later) has a T2 chip, adjust security settings:
Boot into Recovery Mode: Restart and hold Cmd + R.
Open Startup Security Utility > Enter administrator credentials.
Set Secure Boot No Security and Allow Booting from External Media.
Download Ubuntu ISO: Get Ubuntu 22.04 LTS from ubuntu.com. Verify the checksum using: bash Copy shasum ~/Downloads/ubuntu-22.04.4-desktop-amd64.iso Compare with the SHA256 checksum listed on Ubuntu's site.
2. Create a Bootable USB Drive
Insert a USB Drive (8GB+).
Identify the USB Disk: bash Copy diskutil list Note the identifier (e.g., /dev/disk2).
Unmount the Disk: bash Copy diskutil unmountDisk /dev/diskX Replace diskX with your USB identifier.
Write the ISO: bash Copy sudo dd if=~/Downloads/ubuntu-22.04.4-desktop-amd64.iso of=/dev/diskX bs=1m Wait until the process completes.
3. Boot from USB
Insert the USB and restart the Mac Mini.
Hold the Option (Alt) key during boot to access the boot menu.
Select the EFI Boot (orange icon) to start Ubuntu Live Session.
4. Install Ubuntu
Start Installation:
Select language, keyboard layout, and click Install Ubuntu.
Configure Options:
Check Install third-party software for drivers and codecs.
Choose Normal Installation.
Partitioning:
Select Erase disk and install Ubuntu (this deletes macOS and all data).
Ensure the disk uses GPT (for UEFI systems).
Complete Setup:
Set timezone, user account, and password.
Click Install and wait for completion.
5. Post-Installation Steps
Wi-Fi/Drivers:
If Wi-Fi isn t working, connect via Ethernet and run: bash Copy sudo apt update && sudo apt install broadcom-sta-dkms
sudo modprobe wl 
Reboot to apply changes.
Update System: bash Copy sudo apt update && sudo apt upgrade -y 
Function Keys/Brightness:
Install brightnessctl for screen brightness control: bash Copy sudo apt install brightnessctl 
6. Troubleshooting
Boot Issues:
If GRUB doesn t appear, reset NVRAM by holding Cmd + Option + P + R during boot.
T2 Chip Audio:
Some models may require additional steps for audio; check Ubuntu forums for fixes.
Notes
macOS Recovery: Once Ubuntu is installed, macOS cannot be recovered without a backup.
Firmware Updates: Use fwupd to update Mac firmware from Ubuntu: bash Copy sudo apt install fwupd
sudo fwupdmgr refresh --force
sudo fwupdmgr update 
By following these steps, your Mac Mini will run Ubuntu 22.04 LTS exclusively. notes.title: Keto Recipes
notes.body:
+Keto Recipes 
McGriddle muffin 3/10
cinnamon 60g -> 100g
Monkfruit/Sweetleaf - stevia sweetener (or 2 tablespoons regular sugar) 100g
baking powder 50g
almond flour 650g
16 large eggs 18
cream cheese 24 oz
water 1.33cup
vanilla extract   cup 
anise 1 tspn 
lemon extract 1 tbsp -> 2tbsp
mint extract 2 tbsp 
Chaffle
2oz - goat or cream cheese(soft)
1.5c - shredded cheddar 
2 eggs
1tbsp - melted butter
Add Vanilla Extract for breakfast sandwich. 
Coat pan with butter(4  pans)
Combine eggs and cheese until it looks like chunky pancakes
Split between the 4 pans
Bake at 375 for 15 minutes
Flip and bake for another 5 minutes. 

Easy carnivore pancakes:
3 eggs
1 big tablespoon of creamcheese
3 tablespoons of beef collagen
Butter for the pan

Fried Cabbage Balls
1 whole cabbage - chopped and boiled, squeezed dry
1 cup of flour, 1 onion, 6 crushed garlic, 2 eggs, 2 tspn salt/oregano, 2 pinch of pepper/chili flakes. 
Roll into balls and fry 2-3 minutes on each side. 

Sausage 3/11- lost a bunch - 6/9
A
dried sage 50g ground Himalayan salt 60g - 70 ground black pepper 50g - 60 dried marjoram 30 brown sugar/coconut sugar 60g crushed red pepper flakes 1tbsp(mild heat)
ginger powder 10g per 4 pounds ground pork
B
2 teaspoons dried parsley
2 teaspoons Italian seasoning
1 1/2 teaspoons black pepper
2 teaspoons sageh
1 teaspoon thyme
1/2 teaspoon fennel seed (we use whole, but you can crush the   teaspoon of whole seeds if you'd like)
1/2 teaspoon paprika
1 teaspoon red pepper flakes
2 teaspoons salt
1 tablespoon minced garlic (we use fresh if not making a big batch)
1 teaspoon minced onions (we use fresh if not making a big batch)
2 pounds ground beef (or turkey, chicken or pork)
C
2 tbsp sage
1 tsp fennel
1 tsp rosemary
2 tsp black pepper
1 tsp marjoram
2 tbsp salt
1 tbsp brown sugar 
D
4  tbsp sage
2 tsp fennel
1 tsp rosemary
2 tsp black pepper
1 tsp marjoram
2 tbsp salt
1 tbsp brown sugar 
1 tbsp cinnamon
E
8 tbsp sage
2 tsp fennel
3 tsp black pepper
1 tsp marjoram
2 tbsp salt
1 tbsp brown sugar 
1 tbsp cinnamon
1 tsp ginger
1 tbsp red pepper
1 tsp cloves

Keto Crackers
Prep. Preheat the oven. Line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
Mix. Combine the almond flour and sea salt in a large bowl. Add the egg and mix well, until a dough forms. (You can also mix in a food processor.)
Roll. Place the dough between two large pieces of parchment paper. Use a rolling pin roll out the dough into a large rectangle. Cut the cracker dough into smaller rectangles with a pizza cutter or sharp knife. Prick with a fork or toothpick if desired. Place on the lined baking sheet.
Variations
Toasted: Sesame seeds, chia seeds, or poppy seeds would make yummy keto-friendly crackers (or try everything bagel seasoning).
Chopped Herbs: Rosemary, basil, and parsley would all be good for a savory low carb cracker.
Italian: You can transform these crackers with some garlic powder and Italian seasoning.
Mediterranean: With some basil and sun dried tomatoes diced up and baked in. Make sure

notes.title: D-Tools project descriptions
notes.body:
D-Tools project descriptions

The Pre-Wire Installation project involves the strategic placement and installation of necessary wiring infrastructure throughout the property to support various low-voltage systems, including audio, video, networking, lighting control, shading, and security systems. This phase is critical in ensuring that all future technology components can be seamlessly integrated into the home or commercial space, reducing the need for disruptive post-construction work.

notes.body:
Bike Maintenance 
Brakes
Adjust lever all the way out as far from the handle bars as possible
Adjust free stroke all the way in - CW
Remove brake pads
Push pistons in flush with caliper
Insert brake block
Remove bleed screw from top on lever
Feed bleed funnel in lever

notes.title: Plant Facts
notes.body:
4Plant Facts

Harvesting and replanting your hydroponically grown basil can be quite rewarding and ensures a continuous supply of fresh herbs. Here s how you can do both effectively:

### Harvesting Basil
1. **Timing**: Begin to harvest your basil when the plant has at least 6-8 leaves. This usually happens about 5-6 weeks after planting. Regular harvesting encourages the plant to become bushy and produce more leaves.
2. **Method**: Use clean, sharp scissors or your fingers to pinch off the top section of each stem, just above a set of growing leaves. This will encourage the plant to branch from that point. Make sure not to remove more than one-third of the plant at any one time to avoid stressing the basil.
3. **Regrowth**: After each harvest, the basil plant will take some time to regrow leaves. Provide it with adequate nutrients and light to support this growth.

### Replanting Basil
1. **Propagating from Cuttings**:
   - **Cut**: Select a healthy stem about 4 inches long with a few leaves. Cut just below a leaf node (the point on the stem where leaves are attached), as roots grow more easily from these points.
   - **Rooting**: Remove the leaves from the bottom 2 inches of the stem. Place the stem in water, ensuring that no leaves are submerged. Change the water every few days to prevent bacterial growth.
   - **Planting**: Once roots have developed (typically in 2-4 weeks), you can transfer the stem to a new hydroponic system. Use a netted pot and suitable hydroponic medium to support the stem and allow the roots to grow freely.

2. **From Seeds**:
   - If you prefer to start fresh occasionally, basil seeds can also be planted directly in a hydroponic medium like rockwool cubes. Place the seeded cubes in a propagation tray and maintain high humidity and consistent light until the seeds germinate and grow strong enough to be transferred to the main hydroponic system.

### Maintenance Tips
- **Nutrients**: Ensure your nutrient solution is balanced specifically for herbs, particularly with adequate nitrogen for leaf growth.
- **Light**: Basil requires around 10-12 hours of light daily. If you're growing indoors, consider using LED grow lights to provide sufficient energy for growth.
- **Pruning**: Regularly prune your basil plants to prevent them from becoming leggy and to encourage bushier growth.

By following these steps, you can enjoy a continuous cycle of growth, harvest, and replanting of your hydroponic basil.

Bok choy (Pak choi) plants produce seeds in an interesting and quite visible way. Here s how you can identify and harvest seeds from a bok choy plant:

1. **Flowering**: After a period of growth, when the plant matures and the conditions are right (usually in cooler temperatures), bok choy will bolt, or go to seed. This means it sends up a flower stalk from the center of the plant. This is more likely to occur after the plant experiences colder temperatures followed by warmer days.

2. **Seed Pods**: The flowers on the bok choy ar

notes.title: AWS training Summary
notes.body:
AWS training Summary

What is Cloud Computing?
Non-Cloud Services:
Email Server
File Server
Customer Relationship Management (CRM)
Cloud Services:
Gmail
Dropbox
Salesforce.com
Characteristics: Do not own/manage infrastructure, offered on a subscription/consumption model, and scales automatically with demand.
Key Characteristics of Cloud:
On-demand, self-service: Automatic resource consumption without human interaction.
Broad network access: Capabilities available over the internet or WAN.
Resource pooling: Providers serve multiple consumers with pooled resources.
Rapid elasticity: Capabilities can scale based on demand.
Measured service: Resource usage is monitored and metered.
Private Cloud:
Components:
Network & Firewall
Virtualization Cluster
Storage & Backup
Features:
Billing and reporting
Multi-tenancy
Configuration management
Security and identity
Self-service
Service catalog
Service automation
Orchestration
Monitoring and logging
Costs: Data center, security, power, connectivity, physical hardware, software licensing, maintenance, and staff.
Public Cloud:
Benefits:
Variable expense, economies of scale, massive elasticity
Connection: Internet or private link
Components: Compute, Storage, Network, Database
Cloud Service Models:
Private Cloud: Managed by you.
Infrastructure as a Service (IaaS): Managed virtual machines.
Platform as a Service (PaaS): Just bring your code.
Software as a Service (SaaS): Everything is managed for you.
Cloud Service Models: Comparison:
Private Cloud: Manage everything; greater responsibility and control.
IaaS: Manage from the virtual server upwards.
PaaS: Upload code/data to create an application.
SaaS: Consume the service with minimal responsibility and control.
AWS Global Infrastructure:
Region: Separate physical location.
Availability Zone: One or more data centers within a region.
Managing AWS:
Management Console: Web interface for accessing and managing AWS services.
Command Line Interface (CLI):
Example command for launching an EC2 instance: aws ec2 run-instances --image-id ami-xxxxxxxx --count 1 --instance-type t2.micro
Example command for listing S3 bucket contents: aws s3 ls s3://mys3databucket
Code/SDK: Use an IDE with AWS SDK to write code that works with cloud services.
AWS Account Overview
Unique Email Address Required: Each AWS account needs a unique email.
Account Root User: Has full control over the account.
AWS IAM: Used to create users, groups, roles, and policies.
AWS Account Configuration
What You Need:
Credit card for billing
Unique email address (with optional dynamic alias)
AWS account name/alias
Phone for SMS verification
Configuration Steps:
Configure Account Alias
Enable billing access for IAM users
Update billing preferences
Create budget and alarm
Managing AWS
Management Console: Web interface for managing AWS services.
CLI Commands:
Launch EC2 instance: aws ec2 run-instances --image-id ami-xxxxxxxx --count 1 --instance-type t2.micro
List S3 bucket contents: aws s3 ls s3://mys3databucke
notes.summary: AWS training Summary  What is Cloud Computing? Non-Cloud Services: Email Server File Server Customer Relationship Management (CRM) Cloud Services: Gmail Dropbox Salesforce.com Characteristics: Do not

stats
Calculates aggregate statistics, such as average, count, and sum, over the results set.
iplocation
Extracts location information from IP addresses by using 3rd-party databases. This command supports IPv4 and IPv6.
geostats
geostats command is used to generate statistics to display geographic data and summarize the data on maps.
The command generates statistics which are clustered into geographical bins to be rendered on a world map.

notes.title: Jeep Gladiator's HomeLink system
notes.body:
Jeep Gladiator's HomeLink system 

to work with a myQ garage door opener, you'll need to follow these steps. It's important to note that you might need your garage door remote control during this process:

1. **Clear Existing Codes:**
   - Start your Jeep Gladiator and ensure the ignition is on.
   - Press and hold the two outer HomeLink buttons (leaving out the middle one) until the indicator light starts to flash. This may take up to 20 seconds. This step clears any existing settings in the system to prepare for new programming.

2. **Program the HomeLink to your Garage Door Opener:**
   - Hold your garage door remote 1 to 3 inches away from the HomeLink buttons.
   - Simultaneously press the garage door remote button and the HomeLink button you wish to program. Keep pressing until the HomeLink indicator changes from a slow to a rapid blink. This may take up to a minute.
   - Some newer garage door openers are equipped with a "rolling code" feature. If your garage door opener uses this, proceed to the next step.

3. **Rolling Code Feature (if applicable):**
   - After programming the HomeLink system, you'll need to "pair" it with your garage door motor unit to complete the setup for rolling code equipped openers.
   - Locate the "Learn" or "Smart" button on your garage door motor unit. This button is usually found where the hanging antenna wire is attached to the motor unit; the button could be colored red, amber, green, or purple.
   - Press the "Learn" or "Smart" button (you ll have about 30 seconds to initiate the next step).
   - Return to your Jeep Gladiator and press the programmed HomeLink button three times (do this step slowly, pressing and releasing every 2 seconds).
   - The connection is successful if the garage door responds to the Jeep Gladiator's HomeLink button. If not, you might need to repeat the process or ensure you are pressing the HomeLink button firmly.

4. **Testing:**
   - Once programmed, test the functionality by pressing the newly programmed button. Your garage door should respond accordingly.

If your garage door does not program using the steps above, consult the owner's manual for your specific garage door opener or your Jeep Gladiator, as there could be model-specific instructions or additional steps required. Additionally, some newer garage door systems might require additional steps directly related to their own security features. b*H`

notes.title: Microgreens
notes.body:
Microgreens

Pea Shoot microgreens - pair with broccoli and kale for best benefits 
chlorophyll, the thing that makes plants bright green, is a natural body deodorant.
Eat chlorophyll-rich microgreens such as spinach and watercress to counter the smell of broccoli and garlic chives odors.
Microgreens nutrition data
Broccoli microgreens contain more than 550% of the RDA of antioxidant nutrients you need. It has the most complete nutrient profile of any vegetable.
It is packed with Vitamin A, B, C and K, and a sizeable amount of iron, magnesium and phosphorus.
Kale microgreens is one of the latest food trends.
It has a lower antioxidant capacity that broccoli, but has more vitamin B, just as much vitamin C, but less vitamin A than broccoli.
Pea shoot microgreens are an antioxidant like broccoli. Though not as high in nutritional quality as kale or microgreens, eaten together with those two it delivers a lot of vitamin B s: B3(niacin), B1 (thiamin), B2 (riboflavin), B-6 (pyridoxine) and B9 (folate).
They also have double the concentration of iron, phosphorus and magnesium compared to broccoli.
Radish microgreens are the forgotten part of the radish.
Most people eat the root or bulb. It is in the same family as broccoli and kale, Brassicaceae (or mustard), and contains a different set of vitamins and minerals including magnesium, zinc and phosphorus.
And last, but not least, amaranth microgreens, that includes quinoa, is very well balanced in nutrients that include little of Vitamin A, B, and C, and a major source of minerals such as calcium, iron, and magnesium.
Of all the other microgreens, amaranth has the  highest antioxidant capacity.
GS1.org - buy upc labels so you own them. 
Printrunner.com - waterproof labels 
 The purpose of the cold call is to not to sell, but to get a meeting scheduled.  

Always document each tray (type of mg, weight of seeds, date)
Make sure when seeding that it is even
Brocoli - 32g
 
Beginning

notes.title: Ableton Notes
notes.body:
Ableton Notes
MPE -  supercharged MIDI 
Analog - subtractive synthesizer - make complicated sound and pull layers away to get the sound that we want
Physical Model - emulating something in the real world - realistic sounding sounds unless you tinker with physical models
FM(frequency modulation) - can be a free different types depending on how it is set up - you have something that makes sound, and another thing is used to make another sound
Wave tables - new to live 11 - just like serum
Samplers - take audio sample to generate sound - 
Every synthesizer is made of 3 things, and sometimes a 4th - Oscillators, Filters, an Amplifier, and kinda 4th is Envelopes.
Oscillators - the thing that makes sound in a synthesizer determines timber of a sound in conjunction with the filter
Filters - shapes the timber of the sound - pull away sound, filter sounds out to get sound you want - pulls out the high notes- resonator annunciates the highest note that is available 
Amplifier - gives shape to a sound
Envelope - lets us change a parameter over time
LFO - changes the motion of the sound
Always start with signal flow in any synth  - flow is from left to right
Operator - FM Synth but capable of doing types(subtractive, additive, FM)
Wavetables - oscillator works differently, given list of wave forms that can be rapidly switched if wanted or needed. Serum is a wavetable 
Simpler - one shot - best for drum sounds
Sampler - little more control over sound compared to Simpler - 
Midi Effects - change the note not the sound
Arpegiator - used on chords to give them life(great tool to layer a beat)  #D]

notes.title: Music Theory
notes.body:
Music Theory
Interval: The distance between 2 notes.
Intervals we know: Octave, half-step, whole-step
This symbol: b is pronounced "flat" and means a half-step lower.
This symbol: # is pronounced "sharp" and means a half-step higher.
B is middle note in Treble Clef
D is middle note in Bass Clef
 

Chords
Ordered Pitch Class Collections -> Every C, regardless of octave, is a single pitch class
Scale -  ordered series of notes - think about them as a pattern
Each scale has a unique pattern. 
Major - W W H W W W H 
C Major scale is only scale with all white notes
 
 
 Triads - 3 note chords - most basic thing -  ternary harmony  - chords built on thirds
C major triad- 1st,3rd,5th not make C Major Chord(Triad)
Bottom note of triad chord is the root note
Insersion is when a chord has a gap between the notes
Diatonic Chord Progression -    M m m M M m Diminished  
 
Key of C Major
 
Minor 3rd - 1.5 steps above - minor triad
Major 3rd - 2 steps
Diminished Triad (dim)(degrees symbol) - 2 minor 3rds - compressed
Augmented Triad (aug)(+)- 2 major 3rds - stretched out - transition chord

notes.title: Title: Designing an Innovative Wheel Well Insert for Ice Prevention…
notes.body:
Title: Designing an Innovative Wheel Well Insert for Ice Prevention in Winter Climates
Introduction
Winter brings a host of challenges for vehicle owners, particularly in regions where snow and ice are prevalent. One significant issue is the accumulation of snow and ice in the wheel wells of cars, which can impair driving safety and vehicle functionality. Addressing this problem, the concept of a laser-measured, heated wheel well insert emerges as an innovative solution. This essay delves into the purpose, design, and technical considerations of this groundbreaking product aimed at enhancing winter driving safety.
Purpose and Target Audience
The primary purpose of the proposed wheel well insert is to prevent the buildup of snow and ice in the wheel wells of vehicles during winter. Targeting a broad range of vehicles, each insert will be custom-fitted using laser measurement technology to ensure precise fitment for different car brands and models. The universal appeal of this product lies in its potential to significantly improve driving safety and convenience across diverse vehicular segments.
Material Selection and Measurement System
The choice of material for the inserts is pivotal. A rubber molded compound capable of withstanding moderately high temperatures while maintaining flexibility in cold conditions is proposed. This material ensures durability and effectiveness in various environmental conditions. The measurement system for these inserts is twofold: a static machine housed in a warehouse for direct measurement and the use of vehicle blueprints for precise dimensioning. This dual approach ensures accuracy and adaptability for a wide range of vehicles.
Temperature Sensing and Control
Central to the functionality of these inserts is a temperature sensing mechanism. The system activates when ambient temperatures drop to 32 degrees Fahrenheit or lower. A centrally located device, situated between the engine and the inserts, will monitor the temperature, ensuring reliable and efficient operation. This feature automates the process, freeing the driver from manual activation and ensuring the system operates only when necessary.
Heat Sink Integration and Distribution
The integration of a heat sink is a novel aspect of the design. The heat sink will utilize excess heat from the vehicle's engine, repurposing it to warm the wheel well inserts. Various methods can be employed, including direct heat transfer through physical conduits, indirect transfer using a coolant fluid, electrically powered systems, or the innovative use of Thermo-electric Generators (TEGs). Each method has its pros and cons, balancing efficiency, installation complexity, and integration with existing vehicle systems.
For heat distribution within the inserts, a lattice-like grid design molded into the insert is proposed. This design ensures even heat distribution across the entire surface area of the insert. Additionally, a quick-release connection system is envisioned, allowin

notes.title: ChatGPT
notes.body:
ChatGPT
Prompt Engineering
Be Specific
Work in Steps
Iterate and Improve

What  makes a good prompt? 

What is your goal in a concise manner, using relevant keywordsPrompt Formula

I'm looking to explore [subject] in a [format). Do you have any suggestions on [topics] I can cover?

Tabular Format Prompting:
Using tabular format with ChatGPT allows for clear organization and presentation of data, making it easier for the user to analyze and comprehend the output, leading to more accurate understandings and insights.
Prompt Formula
1. (Your question).
2. What are the different categories you can break your answer into for more descriptiveness?
3. Now create one table that includes your original answer with these categories separated into different columns.
Examples
  What are the main factors of growing a Youtube channel?
  Please list the top 5 travel destinations in South America.
Ask Before Answer Prompting:
This framework is a technique to guide ChatGPT to ask for clarification before giving an answer. This helps ensure that the model's answers are as accurate and specific as possible.
Prompt Formula
1. You are an expert in the field of (industry). I'm going to ask you some specific tasks to complete but before you answer, l want you to do the following: If you have any questions about my task or uncertainty about delivering the best answer possible, always ask bullet point questions for clarification before generating your answer. Is that understood?
2. Great, my question is [question). Your task is to [task]. Please ask any questions you have so that I can improve my prompt before you complete your task.
Examples
  Great, my question is how can I drive more sales for my consulting business? Your task is to create a step by step guide for me to implement into my business. Please ask any questions you have so that I can improve my prompt before you complete your task.
  Great, my question is how can I use ChatGPT to maximize my productivity? Your task is to make a weekly schedule Than can yolow. Please ask any questions you nave so that can improve my prompt betore you complete your task.

notes.title: Secrets to living a healthy life
notes.body:
%Secrets to living a healthy life

It's actually simpler than you think:

1. Judge food by how it makes you feel AFTER eating, not during

2. Vitamin D is a scam. You can't reduce the Sun to a pill. Build a solar callous in the summer and eat cod liver during winter.

3. There is no diet. We are human beings. We can eat everything, as long as its prepared properly.

4. PUFA from seafood is healthy and not the same as PUFA from seed oils

5. The quality of your water is as important as the quality of your food

6. Raw milk is best, but lightly pasteurized is also amazing. A little bit of heat isn't the worst thing.

7. Your gut is the most important thing in your body. Ruin your gut, and everything spirals downwards.

8. Drinking warm bone broth on an empty stomach should be prescribed medication

9. Rosemary is one of the best herbs in the world with incredibly underrated neurological benefits

10. Black seed oil is nature's antibiotic. 1/2 tbsp a day gives your immune system an extra shield.

11. Local, raw honey is the best cough syrup

12. Cold pressed seed oils like black seed & high oleic sunflower are actually healthy for you

13. Fearing fruit is stupid. It's even better consumed sun-kissed.

14. Kefir can only be made from kefir grains, and legend says these grains have divine origin

15. Coconut water regulates blood pressure without the side effects of statins

16. Magnesium and potassium deficiency is not talked about enough and one of the root causes of illness

17. Sourdough is the superior form of bread. If you can't tolerate it, you have gut issues.

18. Structuring water is not "woo woo". Throw water in a Flaska and it will taste like spring water. Magical, isn't it?

19. Berries are one of the most potent things you can snack on

20. Liver is a superfood but you should not overeat it. Everything needs to be balanced. Copper : Zinc ratio needs to be in check.

21. Excessive blue light exposure without sunlight is as unhealthy as a fast food meal

22. Most mental illnesses stem from chronic nutritional deficiencies

23. Artificial dyes in candy make children more sick than sugar itself

24. While sugar is not the enemy and it's good to peat, don't overdo it. Eating ice cream 3x per day is not healthy.

25. Eating alfresco extends your lifespan

26. Saying prayer over your food increases bioavailability

27. Cows are net positives to the world, and the greenhouse gases they emit do not cause a problem

28. Every animal you eat is a sacrifice. Eating animals slain in inhumane conditions brings bad energy into your life.

29. A bee is the best pet you can have. You give them a home, they give you honey, comb and propolis for a lifetime. Friends!

30. Do parasite cleanses. But make sure you take activated charcoal or a binder to remove the toxins you might have just activated.

31. Gut problems start in the mouth. It's the beginning of the digestive track, after all.

32. Caffeine is a miracle drug, but you have to use it and not let i

notes.title: apt-get update
notes.body:
apt-get update
		 
		 
		apt-get install \
		apt-transport-https \
		ca-certificates \
		curl \
		gnupg \
		lsb-release
		 
		 
		curl -fsSL https://download.docker.com/linux/ubuntu/gpg | sudo gpg --dearmor -o /usr/share/keyrings/docker-archive-keyring.gpg
		 
		 
		echo \
		"deb [arch=amd64 signed-by=/usr/share/keyrings/docker-archive-keyring.gpg] https://download.docker.com/linux/ubuntu \
		$(lsb_release -cs) stable" | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/docker.list > /dev/null
		 
		 
		apt-get update
		apt-get install docker-ce docker-ce-cli containerd.io
		 
		systemctl status docker
		docker info
 ":
 {kv 'CM8 #D]

notes.title: Security+ (SYO-601)
notes.body:
Security+ (SYO-601)
Threats, Attacks and Vulnerabilities 
Architecture and design
Implementation
Operation and Incident Response
Governance, Risk, and Compliance

Risk - is the likelihood of a threat actor taking advantage of a vulnerability by using a threat against an IT asset
Asset - any part of an IT infrastructure that has value
Likelihood - the possibility of assists being damaged over time
Threat Actor -anyone or anything with the motive and resources to attack another IT Infrastructure - Hacker, Hacktivist, Script kiddies, Insiders, Competitors, Shadow IT, Criminal Syndicates, State Actor, Advanced Persistent Threat(APT)
Vulnerability - a weakness in a assest
Threat - an action that a threat actor can use again a vulnerability to cause harm
Exploits take advantage of vulnerabilities
Threat actors are the sources of threats
Attack Vectors - pathways to gain access to restricted systems
 
Threat Intelligence sources - facilitate risk management, hardening can reduce incident response time, 
OSINT(open-source intelligence) - refers to public cyber security intelligence sources - CVE(Common Vulnerabilities and exposures)
STIX is a cybersecurity intelligence (CI) sharing format: TAXII exchanges CI
RMFs(risk management frameworks) - provide guidance on identifying and managing risk
Security controls mitigate specific threats
Managerial Security Controls include admin functions like background checks
Operational Security Controls include policy review
Technical Security Controls relate to specific IT security solutions
Security Control types - physical, detective, corrective, preventive, deterrent, and compensating
A risk assessment strives to determine the likelihood and impact of threats
Risk types include environment, person made, internal, and external
Risk treatments(management) include acceptance, mitigation, transference, and avoidance
SLE = AV x EF
ALE = SLE x ARO
A qualitative risk assessment is based on subjective risk severity levels
A risk register is an up-to-date centralized list of risks and their relative seventies and mitigations
Risk heat maps and risk matrices are used to plot and chart risk severity levels
BIA(business impact analysis) - Financial(loss on contracts of fines), Reputation, Data Loss
MTBF(mean time between failures) - average time between repairable component failures - software patching
MTTF(mean time to failure) - average time between non-repairable component failures - hard disks, switches, routers
MTTR(mean time to repair) - time required to repair a failed component
PTA (privacy threshold assessment)
RPO(recovery point objective) - maximum tolerable amount of data loss - directly rated to backup frequency
RTO(recovery time objective) -  maximum tolerable amount of downtime - returns systems and data to usable state
Data Classification - Government/Military (Top secret, secret, confidential) PII personally identifiable info, PHI , proprietary , public/private, critical, financial
Tools - any method of applyi
notes.summary: Security+ (SYO-601) Threats, Attacks and Vulnerabilities  Architecture and design Implementation Operation and Incident Response Governance, Risk, and Compliance  Risk - is the likelihood of a threat

Protocol - contain the definition or a specific device

notes.title: Network+ Notes(N10-007)
notes.body:
iNetwork+ Notes(N10-007)
Models - OSI and TCP/IP
OSI 7 layer model
1 Physical - cables and connections
2 Data - Mac Addresses
3 Network - Logical addresses - routers
4 Transport - disassembles packets 
5 Session - type of connection
6 Presentation - converts data for applications to read
7 Application - background process that allows the application to be network aware
TCP/IP
1 Network Interface - Hardware and cabling
2 Internet - IP addresses
3 Transport - assembly/dis and connections to other applications
4 Application - 

OSI
Outbound
Physical and Data - Mac addresses stripped and frame check sequence checked
Network - holds on to return IP and TCP segment left for transport
Transport - checks sequencing number and sends port# and data to 5/6/7 OSI and Application TCP/IP
Session - Connect a server to a client on a remote system
Presentation - not really used anymore
Application - backbone of the application that allows them to interface to the network, return# held in memory for future data to be sent back

Mac Addresses - 48-bit identifier 
CRC- cyclic redundancy check
Unicast is sent to a single device
Broadcast is sent to all devices and represented by all F s in the MAC address 

All data is sent in FRAMES

Topology - the organization of 1 s and 0 s in the form of frames, move from host to host

(Wired Networks)
Bus Topology - all hosts are connected to a single chunk of cable - basically obsolete
Ring Topology - single ring of cabling, no ends. Created by IBM
Star Topology - plugged into a central block in the center.  Considered hybrid topology 
Hybrid Topology
Star Bus - Classic bus shrunk down into a Hub/Switch - Primary topology that we use today
(Wireless Networks)
Mesh Topology - Every device is connected to each other. - Fully Meshed Topology
Partially Meshed Topology - used in WiFi networks 
Spine and Leaf - used in data centers to overcome the limitations of the traditional three-tier architecture. Spine and leaf topology offers benefits such as improved redundancy, increased bandwidth, improved scalability, lower costs, low latency and congestion avoidance
east-west traffic (server-to-server communication)
north-south traffic (client-to-server communication)

Coax Cable - oldest form of networking cable
RG - radio grade - define thickness of cable, thickness of conductor, and OHMs
RG-58 50 OHM - oldest form of coax
RG-59 75 OHM/RG-6 75 OHM - RG-6 is the predecessor of RG-59

Twisted Pair Cabling - most common form of cabling we see on networks.
UTP - no shielding, keep away from any kind of interference (heat, electric, magnetic fields)
STP - has a shield to protect from interference 
RJ-45 - used for terminating on ethernet - 8P8C

Cat Ratings
Cat1 - Tele - 4 wire
Cat3 - oldest form - 10MB network
Cat5 - 100MB/s ~100m
Cat5e - 100-1000Mb/s ~100m
Cat6 - 1Gb/s ~100m 10Gb/s ~55m
Cat6a - 10Gb/s ~100m
Cat7 - 10Gb/s ~100m

Fiber Optic Cabling
Core - Cladding - Cable Jacket
Multimode - propagate light, carry LED signals - almost always

NFT s

6 easy steps of buying, creating, and selling NFT s
Select NFT Marketplace and open an account. (Opensea.io, Rarible.com, Mintable.app )
Connect your MetaMask wallet to your account and add funds
Fund your metamask wallet with required there to other crypto
 explores other NFT options available and select the NFT you wish to buy
Place your bid for the NFT following marketplace rules
Depending on the amount you bid, you may or may not get the NFT. Highest bidder wins
Creating and Selling
Select NFT marketplace that lets you create NFT s and open an account
Connect your MetaMask wallet to your account and add funds to it
Prepare the content you wish to  mint  as an NFT and upload to your account
Pay required amount to the marketplace to create/mint your NFT
Select NFT you wish to sell and help promote it via social media
Buyers will bid on your NFT. Once a buyer buys your NFT, you will receive payment to your wallet
Installing MetaMask - wallet that serves as a gateway to the different blockchain apps - browser extension/mobile app
Polygon - used for listing NFTs - no gas fees
Creating a collection on Polygon - create a new collection in OpenSea - limited to selling to people who have ether in Polygon - DAI, USDC, ETHER

notes.body:
IT Specialist SRs


/extract:[path]: Extracts the content of the package to the path folder. If a path isn t specified, then a Browse dialog box appears.
/log:[path to log file]: Enables verbose logging (more detailed information recorded in the log file) for the update installation.
/lang:lcid: Sets the user interface to the specified locale when multiple locales are available in the package.
/quiet: Runs the package in silent mode.
/passive: Runs the update without any interaction from the user.
/norestart: Prevents prompting of the user when a restart of the computer is needed.
/forcerestart: Forces a restart of the computer as soon as the update is finished.
You can always type /?, /h, or /help from the command line to view these options. 
Resources for more information
Installation Package: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/win32/msi/installation-package
App packager (MakeAppx.exe): https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/win32/appxpkg/make-appx-package--makeappx-exe-
Portable Executables: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/win32/debug/pe-format
Self-extractor: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/troubleshoot/windows-client/deployment/command-switches-supported-by-self-extractor-package
If you're interested in downloading 7-Zip, check out the link here.
For more information about creating an archive or zipped file using Windows PowerShell, check out the link here.
For more information about the Linux tar command, please check out the following link here.
A few common DLLs used by Windows include:
.drv files - Device drivers manage the operation of physical devices such as printers.
.ocx files - Active X controls provide controls like the program object for selecting a date from a calendar.
.cpl files - Control panel files manage each of the functions found in the Windows Control Panel. 
DLL dependencies can be broken when: 
Overwriting DLL dependencies - It is possible for an application to overwrite the DLL dependency of another app, causing the other app to fail. 
Deleting DLL files - Some applications and malware may delete the DLLs needed by other applications installed on a system. 
Applying upgrades or fixes to DLLs - Can cause a problem called  DLL hell  where an application installs a new version of the shared DLL for a computer system. However, other applications that are dependent on the shared DLL have not yet been updated to be compatible with the new version of the DLL. This causes the other applications to fail when the end user tries to launch them. 
Rolling-back to previous DLL versions - A user may try to reinstall an older application that stopped working after a shared DLL file was upgraded by a newer app. However, the reinstallation of the app that uses the old DLL version can overwrite the new DLL file. This DLL version roll-back can cause the newer app with the shared DLL dependency to fail the next time it tries to run. 
Microsoft has remedied these problems through the use of:
Windows File Protection - The

notes.title: Door code if locked is 5198
notes.body:
Door code if locked is 5198
I ll be back Monday late afternoon!

Roddick - white dog
Keelee - black dog

FOOD - White bin in kitchen, dishes and Stella In bin
They both get a scoop of food in the AM (around 7/8A) and PM (5/6P)- with a half of a Stella patty 
Bin of treats in bag by door as well

WATER
They go they water fast- there is a big jug in kitchen and also I packed a small dish in bag by door

MEDs - in bag by door
Roddick - gets 1 of the CBD treats in AM & PM 
Keelee - gets 1 pill ( which cheese in purple Tupperware in fridge) in AM & 1 CBD in PM
***do not over feed CBD treats they are a very high dose!

INJURY
Roddick has injured his acl a couple times so ideally just 15min a day of shot frisbee throws (avoid deep snow)
Keelee broke her leg when she was a puppy so she has pretty bad Arthritis now

BATHROOM
Dogs are both trained on  park time  which tells them to use the bathroom 
Don t play with Roddick until he uses the bathroom otherwise he won t go
Both dogs need to go out a couple times a day starting at 7A & ending at 9:30P or later

WALKS & LEASHES
The dogs can go on 1-2 short (1-2mile or 30min- 1hr) walks a day - Miller ranch, Edwards preserve or short berry creek loop are great
The dogs pull, sorry about that but they are great off leash when you can do that

COMMANDS
Use Name + command word
Normal  Come Stay Sit 
They also  Heal  really well
 Down  is to lay down
 Off  is to get off of something
 Load up  is to get in car
 Leave it  ideally with hand signal tells them to stop doing what ever they are doing including throwing a stick
**AVOID - Throwing any sticks 

VET 
Vail Valley Veterinary in Edwards - 970-926-3495
**please call me before going unless emergency**

PICTURES & TEXT
Please send me pictures and text updates when you can, I may also bother you for more :)

HOUSE- 
Let me know if you decided to stay at the apartment - clean sheets are on bed & towels are clean in bathroom

- Dog beds for dogs are in the living room to take with you to your place mv"

notes.title: Car detailing
notes.body:
Car detailing
Exterior

Pre rinse car(top to bottom)  de-ionized water =spot free water
2 bucket system of washing(grit guard for bottom of bucket)eco wash system?
Soap car from top down on all smooth surfaces
Pressure wash soap off
Spray wheel wells with degreasers to break up hard dirt with pressure washer
Degrease wheels/tires
Pressure wash
Detailing brushes to get in all nooks and crannies
Pressure wash again
Spray ph balanced degreaser on all plastics and any part that may still have gunk on them. 
Gently scrub with scrub brush(be careful of paint surface)
Power wash again. 
Continue previous steps as needed
Car exterior is finished
Open doors
Spray Mild ph neutral degreaser on all newly shown surfaces
Spray water to rinse, then dry the surfaces 
Dry vehicle to 80%
Add lubricant to paint(pink wipeout)
Decontamination process(clay towel) wipe till paint feels smooth
Dry the vehicle completely(compressed air?) microfiber towels
Ceramic pro for further polish products

Interior
Vacuum car
Use compressed air to blow out any gunk that didn t budge
Vacuum car again
Starting top to bottom, clean entire interior. (Industrial Steam cleaner) soaps and detergents
Clean carpets. (Industrial carpet cleaner)
 
Detail Interior
Hand brush
Leather Cleaner
All Purpose Cleaner
PH neutral products
Micro fiber cloths(high quality)
Air gun attachment (cone with spring in middle)
Industrial steamer



 ;(\ -6J&
